Hearth: Needs Attention panel scroll loses items like Workers did (Forge-e0c) (#80)

* fix: Hearth Needs Attention panel scroll loses items

Apply the same viewport offset pattern (separate view start from
selection cursor) that was used to fix Workers scrolling. The panel
now tracks a needsAttentionViewStart offset so scrolling down no
longer causes items at the top to disappear permanently.

Also properly clamp the selection cursor when the items list shrinks,
matching the existing Workers panel behavior.

Bead: Forge-e0c

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-e0c

Clamp needsAttentionViewStart to prevent the viewport from hiding items
unnecessarily when the list shrinks — reset to 0 when everything fits,
otherwise cap at total-maxItems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[book-02] revise ch-13: training origin fix, apple order, 13+ the-way cuts, Samuel complication

Critical: Harper came to orchard independently (not through Clara); "Not out of fear.
Out of choice" removed from training context (belongs to voyage request, book-01 ch-17).
Apple ripening corrected: Greenings → Baldwins → Northern Spies. "The way" reduced
13+ → 2. Tell-after-show on "Thank you" cut (2 paragraphs → 1 sentence). Samuel
complication: sits down beside Lily — unmeasured action becomes final exhibit. Weight
reduced 13 → ~4. Backstory thinned (ch-11 redundancy). Two-records anaphora cut.
Harper humanized (pipe detail). Exit differentiated: forward motion, "a man who sat down."
Bible: harper.md (teaching quotes, method, sequence), lily-montgomery.md (training
origin clarification, Samuel measurement, Northern Spies spelling).
3,930 → 3,496 words (-434 net).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: wire asynq worker server + scheduler (#16)

* feat: wire asynq worker server + scheduler into main.go

The worker framework was fully implemented but never started.
Now main.go launches:
- asynq worker server (processes cleanup, email, stats jobs)
- asynq scheduler (enqueues periodic tasks: peer cleanup every
  15min, stats recalc every hour)

Both shut down gracefully on SIGINT/SIGTERM alongside the HTTP
server. Requires Redis (already running via docker-compose).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: atomic password reset token claim, UNIQUE index, token cleanup

Security fixes from reviewer:
- Replaced GetByTokenHash + MarkUsed with atomic ClaimByTokenHash
  (UPDATE ... SET used=true WHERE used=false RETURNING) to prevent
  TOCTOU race condition on token redemption
- Token is now claimed BEFORE changing password (was wrong order)
- Migration 014: token_hash index changed to UNIQUE
- Expired/used tokens cleaned up by the periodic cleanup handler
  (DELETE WHERE used=true OR expires_at < NOW()-7days)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: NewAuthService now requires PasswordResetStore parameter

Removed the hardcoded NewMemoryPasswordResetStore() from
NewAuthService constructor. All callers (production and tests)
now explicitly pass the store. Production uses postgres impl,
tests use memory impl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add BE-1.2.3 task to move test doubles out of domain code

MemorySessionStore, MemoryPasswordResetStore, and NoopSender live
in the service package but are only used by tests. Domain code
should only define interfaces, not test implementations. New task
to move them to a testutil package.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update resume doc for session continuity

commit.message:
Alpha Cycle V3: data layer + cycle setup screen enhancements

Phase 1: Remove legacy code (old usecases, calculators, signal detector)
Phase 2: New data layer with Cycle/Trade models, V3 repositories (cycles_v3, trades_v3)
- Hive models with proper defaultValue for migration safety
- CycleRepository/TradeRepository with signal computation
- Provider layer: cycle/trade/portfolio providers rewritten for V3
- Unified portfolio provider supporting both strategies

Cycle setup screen improvements:
- Fix seed input instability (remove inputFormatters, use FocusNode-based formatting)
- Add Korean amount reading below input (천만원, 삼억원 etc.)
- Show current price + change rate next to selected ticker
- Dollar conversion + share count for 무한매수법
- Responsive ticker/price layout with Wrap
- Real-time calculation preview

History screen: add summary card with total invested/recovered/PnL
Portfolio chart: persist custom colors to Hive settings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add supplemental npm packages installed during setup

Introduces EXTRA_PACKAGES list for npm packages not tied to a
specific tool, starting with pyright.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Budvik e-commerce portal: full implementation

- Auth with roles (Admin/Sales/Client), NextAuth + JWT
- Product catalog with 50 seeded items across 6 categories
- Order system with status tracking (6 statuses)
- Loyalty system "Болти" (5% cashback)
- Admin panel: products CRUD, users, sales managers, orders
- 1C integration: CommerceML XML + CSV import/export
- AI features: smart search, recommendations, chat widget
- PWA: manifest, service worker, mobile bottom nav
- PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stale worker detection should exclude bellows/lifecycle workers (Forge-hhx) (#85)

* fix: exclude bellows/lifecycle workers from stale detection

Bellows, cifix, and reviewfix workers are long-running background
processes that only produce log output when external state changes
(e.g. PR events). They can be legitimately silent for long stretches,
so the stale worker detection was incorrectly flagging them as stuck.

Add a phase exclusion filter (matching the existing pattern in
ActiveDispatchWorkers) to StalledWorkers so these phases are skipped.

Bead: Forge-hhx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-hhx

Centralize the background phase list ('bellows', 'cifix', 'reviewfix')
into a single `backgroundPhases` constant in internal/state/db.go.
Reuse it across StalledWorkers, ActiveDispatchWorkers, and
ActiveDispatchWorkersByAnvil to prevent the lists from drifting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add atom-specific scattering form factors (#478)

* Add atom-specific scattering form factors

- Add scattering_f0 property to AtomData (defaults to 1.0)
- Create FormFactorAtomicConstant class using atom-specific form factors
- Normalize scattering intensity by sum of squared form factors (Σfᵢ²)
- Update ScatteringFunction to use Scatterer struct with position and atom id
- Add tests for new form factor functionality
- Update documentation in analysis.md and topology.md

Backwards compatible: when scattering_f0=1.0 (default), normalization
by Σfᵢ² equals normalization by N, giving identical results.

* Address scattering review: hoist q.norm(), fix div-by-zero, align docs

- Hoist q.norm() computation outside scatterer loops in PBC and IPBC
- Collapse identical SIMD/EIGEN/GENERIC branches into single loop
- Use atoms[] with assert instead of atoms.at() in hot loop
- Guard against division by zero when sum of squared form factors is zero
- Fix LaTeX summation limits and align explicit scheme notation with Debye

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: timeout 6s si Firebase auth ne répond pas → affiche login

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add FLUX.1-schnell automatic fallback when FLUX.1-dev is down (#40)

When FLUX.1-dev returns persistent 500/503 errors (server outage),
automatically fall back to FLUX.1-schnell (4 steps) which uses a
different endpoint and may still be online. This prevents complete
generation failure during FLUX.1-dev outages.

Fallback chain: FLUX.1-dev (5 retries) → without init_image → FLUX.1-schnell → fail

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Penn crimson+blue visual overhaul with spotlight rotation

Design:
- Animated hero gradient cycling navy↔crimson (20s)
- Text shimmer on "AI, Applied." headline after reveal
- Mixed crimson+blue network nodes in hero
- Crimson stats bar numbers with gradient dividers

Cards:
- Spotlight cards: crimson conic gradient border, crimson badges/CTAs
- Standard cards: blue gradient border on hover
- Clear "ALL USE CASES" divider between sections
- Von Restorff effect: spotlights visually distinct from standard

Spotlight algorithm:
- Top 2 contributors get 2 reserved slots each (4 total)
- Remaining 8 slots: round-robin across other contributors
- All 12 spotlight slots rotate on each page load
- Max contributor diversity via round-robin distribution

UX improvements:
- Crimson active filter pills (larger padding, Fitts's Law)
- Scroll animation timing ≤400ms (Doherty Threshold)
- Section headers with crimson left border on detail pages
- Staggered card entrance with scale transform

commit.message:
feat: 릴스 포스팅 1시간 후 자동 스토리 공유

- uploader/instagram.py: post_video_story() 추가 (video_url → STORIES)
- story_dispatcher.py: 큐 파일(output/story_queue.json) 기반 지연 스토리 포스팅
  - enqueue_story(): 릴스 URL + 1시간 딜레이를 큐에 추가
  - run(): 예약 시각 된 항목 게시, 실패 시 최대 3회 재시도
- dispatch.py / main.py: 릴스 포스팅 직후 enqueue_story() 호출
- deploy/crontab.txt: */5 * * * * story_dispatcher.py 크론 추가

흐름: 릴스 포스팅 → 큐 저장 → 5분마다 크론 체크 → 1시간 후 스토리 자동 게시

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement items 13-15: logging, remove globals, fetch error handling

- Item 13: Replace print() with logging module in network_summary.py.
  CLI scans configure basicConfig(INFO) so alert/device events appear
  with timestamps; dashboard imports don't configure logging so INFO
  messages stay silent there. Remove verbose parameter from
  process_scan_alerts() — logging level now controls output.

- Item 14: Remove in-memory globals (latest_summary, latest_analysis).
  home() and /chat now call load_latest_summary() on each request so
  the dashboard always reflects the latest DB state without needing a
  process restart or global mutation.

- Item 15: Add r.ok checks and .catch() handlers to all JS fetch()
  chains (device table, scan history, metrics/charts, device modal).
  Each failure shows a user-visible error message instead of a silent
  broken state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: render tutor messages with markdown + math instead of plain text

The homework chat was rendering tutor responses as plain text with
whitespace-pre-wrap, so markdown formatting (**bold**, lists, etc.)
and LaTeX math ($...$) showed as raw characters. Now uses the existing
MarkdownWithMath component for tutor messages, giving proper formatted
step-by-step solutions with bold headers and rendered equations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Scheduled govulncheck security scanning with bead creation (Forge-o3t) (#77)

* feat: scheduled govulncheck security scanning with bead creation

Add a vulncheck package that runs govulncheck on Go-based anvils and
auto-creates beads for discovered vulnerabilities. Priority is mapped
from severity: P1 (critical), P2 (high), P3 (medium), P4 (low).

Features:
- Scheduled scanning as a daemon goroutine (configurable interval,
  default 24h, set to 0 to disable)
- On-demand scanning via 'forge scan' CLI subcommand
- Bead descriptions include CVE IDs, affected packages, upgrade paths,
  and remediation steps
- Deduplication: skips creating beads if one already references the
  vuln ID
- Only reports vulnerabilities with actual call-stack findings (not
  just imported-but-unused modules)

Config: settings.vulncheck_interval (default "24h", 0 to disable)

Bead: Forge-o3t

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-o3t

- Remove unused exported types Vuln, Module, Version, Pkg (dead code)
- Add MarshalJSON to ScanResult for proper error serialization in JSON output
- Add Aliases field to osvEntry; extract CVEs from aliases (GO- IDs never start with CVE-)
- Populate ParsedVuln.Symbols from ecosystem_specific.imports in OSV data
- Fix beadExists to propagate context cancellation instead of swallowing it
- Remove duplicate truncateSummary in scan.go; use existing truncate from history.go
- Move CreateBeads before JSON output branch so --json still creates beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-o3t

- beadExists now uses `bd list --status=open --json` instead of `bd search`
  so closed/resolved vulnerability beads do not suppress re-creation if the
  vuln reappears

- scanAnvil wraps the govulncheck subprocess with context.WithTimeout so a
  slow or hung network connection cannot block the goroutine indefinitely
- Pass VulncheckTimeout through vulncheck.New() in both daemon and forge scan

commit.message:
Add live game sharing system with real-time collaborative scoring

- vercel.json: SPA rewrites so /game/:token serves the app
- SharedGameRoute: loads game by share_token, subscribes to Realtime UPDATE
- SharedGameView: public read-only scorecard (no auth), full ScoreSymbol grid
- JoinGameScreen: enter name → added to game.players, scores synced live
- Joined player scoring UI: +/- per hole, debounced Supabase UPDATE
- ScorecardScreen: "Invita" button (Web Share API / clipboard fallback)
- handleGameStart: generates share_token (crypto.randomUUID), inserts with share_mode='play'
- liveShareToken state stored and passed to ScorecardScreen

SQL to run in Supabase:
  ALTER TABLE games ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_token text UNIQUE DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()::text;
  ALTER TABLE games ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_mode text DEFAULT 'view';
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS games_share_token_idx ON games(share_token);
  CREATE POLICY "Join live game by share" ON games FOR UPDATE TO anon
    USING (is_live = true AND share_mode = 'play')
    WITH CHECK (is_live = true AND share_mode = 'play');

commit.message:
Add python-toolkit plugin: opinionated Python development harness

Baseline: Python 3.12+ / uv / ruff / mypy (strict) / pytest / hatch

Core: 12 non-negotiable architecture rules for large codebases
(one entrypoint, pure imports, types everywhere, explicit boundaries,
immutable data, no ad-hoc globals, exception taxonomy, side effects
at edges, layered testing, auto-format, locked deps, no dynamic magic)

Components:
- 4 skills: architecture, coding-standards, testing, bash
- 3 agents: architecture-reviewer, test-writer, library-lookup
- 1 script: scaffold-project.sh (creates full project skeleton)
- 1 hook: auto-format Python files with ruff on edit
- 48 pinned library references (generating, will follow)

CLAUDE.md (~135 lines) stays lean: 12 rules + library version table.
Skills load on trigger. Library references load on demand (one at a time).
Agents run in isolated context to protect main conversation.

fix: resolve tiptap collaboration crash from mismatched ySyncPluginKey instances

The "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'doc')" crash was caused by
@tiptap/extension-collaboration (v3) using ySyncPlugin from @tiptap/y-tiptap
while @tiptap/extension-collaboration-cursor used yCursorPlugin from y-prosemirror.
Both packages create separate PluginKey('y-sync') instances, so the cursor plugin
couldn't find the sync plugin's state. Fixed by aliasing y-prosemirror to
@tiptap/y-tiptap in Turbopack/webpack config, and upgrading all tiptap packages
to consistent v3 versions.

commit.message:
Simplify repo: merge infra stages, consolidate repos (#74)

* Simplify repo structure: merge infrastructure stages, consolidate repos

- Merge infrastructure/controllers + infrastructure/configs into flat
  infrastructure/<service>/ directories (eliminates controllers/configs split
  that caused middleware placement bugs like PR #59)
- Reduce Flux stages from 4 to 3: repositories → infrastructure → apps
- Consolidate 10 individual Helm repository files into single multi-doc YAML
- Remove redundant docs (README.md, architecture.md, infrastructure.md, apps.md)
- Update CI workflow, CLAUDE.md, README.md for new structure

All kustomize build outputs verified identical before/after.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Keep old Flux Kustomizations with prune: false for safe migration

The old infrastructure-controllers and infrastructure-configs Kustomizations
have prune: true with finalizers. Deleting them outright would trigger
garbage collection of all managed resources (HelmReleases, middlewares, etc.),
causing cluster-wide downtime.

Instead, keep them temporarily with prune: false pointing to the new
./infrastructure path. This removes their finalizers safely. They can be
deleted in a follow-up PR after the new infrastructure Kustomization has
reconciled and taken ownership.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v1.8.1: fix hasAttachment filter, add list_attachments tool

hasAttachment filter:
- iCloud IMAP does not support SEARCH HEADER or reliable LARGER filtering
  for attachment detection — previous implementation was silently broken
- Now implemented as client-side BODYSTRUCTURE post-filter using
  findAttachments() — accurate but requires other filters to narrow the
  candidate set to <500 emails first (enforced with clear error message)
- Applied consistently in countEmails, searchEmails, bulkMove, bulkDelete

list_attachments tool (new):
- Fetches BODYSTRUCTURE, walks MIME tree via findAttachments()
- Returns partId, filename, mimeType, size, disposition per attachment
- Verified live: Anthropic invoice email correctly returned 2 PDF
  attachments (Invoice + Receipt), Spotify receipt returned 1 PDF
- 40/40 tests passing

commit.message:
fix(banking): support ICICI PDF bank statement parsing

parsePDFContent previously only matched Kotak's format (serial number
prefix + "DD MMM YYYY" date). ICICI statements use "DD/MM/YYYY" dates
with no serial number prefix, causing all lines to be skipped and the
"parse: all_failed - no transactions extracted" error.

Fix:
- Refactored parsePDFContent into two passes:
  PASS 1 (Kotak): serial-number prefix + "DD MMM YYYY" date (unchanged)
  PASS 2 (ICICI/HDFC/SBI): "DD/MM/YYYY" or "DD-MM-YYYY" date at line
  start, optional second value-date stripped, then amount extraction
- Extracted shared helpers: classifyByDescription(), extractAmountsAndDesc(),
  shouldSkipLine() to avoid duplication between the two passes
- Both passes emit the same column names so existing buildColumnMapping /
  normalizeRow pipeline handles them identically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update directory links to directory.mirajoco.org/search_results

- Add Tier Range column to README Models table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: SMT-LIB2 proof backend for Z3 integration

Translates proof obligations to SMT-LIB2 format and invokes Z3 as a
subprocess. Handles arithmetic, boolean logic, quantifiers, implications,
field access, and conservation laws. Gracefully falls back to Unverified
when Z3 is not installed. Registered as higher-priority backend in CLI.
Closes #25.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: NuMatch keşfe katıl butonu mobilde görünür hale getirildi

Optin ekranında overflow-y-auto eklendi, padding ve spacing
azaltıldı, mt-auto/mb-auto ile dikey ortalama korundu.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: fix building-site words and add multi-answer support

Updated building-site to correct 24 words. Words with alternative
names (e.g., "cement-mixer" / "concrete mixer") now accept multiple
correct answers. Word format supports string (single answer) or
array (first element = display name, all elements accepted).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial chromebook dev environment setup

Brewfile, setup script, and install scripts for a complete dev
environment on a new Chromebook including brew, Java/SDKMan, Python,
Node, Terraform, Docker, pre-commit, VS Code, IntelliJ, Claude Code,
and Git.
